article_title: New Research Shows AI Is Splitting the Labor Market in Two, with Entry-Level Workers Bearing the Brunt

content_preview.title: New Research Shows AI Is Splitting the Labor Market in Two, with Entry-Level Workers Bearing the Brunt
content_preview.summary: Federal Reserve and Harvard studies reveal AI is simultaneously eliminating entry-level positions and boosting wages for experienced workers, creating a bifurcated labor market as tech layoffs surpass 45,000 in early 2026.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

A convergence of new research from the Federal Reserve Bank of Dallas and Harvard Business School is revealing how generative AI tools are reshaping the labor market along a sharp dividing line: experience. Rather than displacing workers uniformly, AI appears to be creating a two-tier workforce in which seasoned professionals see rising wages and expanded roles while entry-level workers face shrinking opportunities and stagnant pay. The findings arrive as tech layoffs have already [...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.source_verification: Dallas Fed study confirms: employment in top 10% AI-exposed sectors declined 1%, computer systems design wages up 16.7% vs 7.5% national average, codifiable vs tacit knowledge framework, J. Scott Davis as author. HBR confirms Suraj Srinivasan study: 13% decline in automation-prone postings, 20% increase in analytical roles, 7% skill reduction, Srinivasan quote on augmentation vs elimination.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All specific figures verified: 2.5% total employment growth, 1% decline in AI-exposed sectors, 5% decline in computer systems design jobs, 16.7% wage growth, 40% median experience premium, 0.9pp drop in 20-24 worker share (16.4% to 15.5%). Ireland data point (20% drop) attributed to Fortune correctly.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Excellent analytical tone appropriate for the Analysis category. Data-driven without editorializing. The analysis section adds valuable context about Block layoffs and strategic restructuring without taking sides.
editor_notes.originality: Outstanding synthesis of multiple research papers into a coherent narrative about AI's bifurcated labor market impact. The codifiable vs tacit knowledge framework is well-explained and provides the conceptual backbone.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Exceptional analysis piece at 1129 words. Five authoritative sources including Federal Reserve research and Harvard Business Review. One of the strongest analytical articles in this batch.
